title: State Street CEO: ETFs Have 'Exploded'
summary: State Street Chairman and CEO Ron O'Hanley discusses the company's strategic approach to the exploding ETF market, 'episodic' M&A, and the impacts of geopolitical risks in the markets. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Westpac Profit Misses Estimates as CEO Flags Mideast Risks
summary: Westpac Banking Corp.’s first-half profit missed expectations as Chief Executive Officer Anthony Miller flagged worries about the impact of the Middle East war on customers.

title: Investor Michael Burry Says He Exited Entire GameStop Position
summary: Famed investor Michael Burry sold his entire position in GameStop Corp. after the gaming retailer made an offer to buy e-commerce company eBay Inc. for about $56 billion in cash and stock.

title: Apollo-Owned Auto Supplier Tenneco Is Said to Tap Banks for IPO
summary: Tenneco Inc. has picked banks to lead its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, as the automotive supplier advances a public listing four years after it was acquired by Apollo Global Management Inc.

title: Musk Agrees to Pay $1.5 Million Over SEC Twitter Stake Case
summary: Elon Musk agreed to pay $1.5 million to settle Securities and Exchange Commission allegations that he cheated Twitter shareholders in 2022 by failing to properly disclose his growing stake in the social media company.
